content:
<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

## claude.ai Linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

## plugin:context7-plugin:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

- brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- playwright-cli: Automates browser interactions for web testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction. Use when the user needs to navigate websites, interact with web pages, fill forms, take screenshots, test web applications, or extract information from web pages.
- receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification
- using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- dioxus: Dioxus 0.7 reactive UI framework reference (covers dioxus, dioxus-hooks, dioxus-signals, dioxus-stores, dioxus-router, dioxus-fullstack, dioxus-document). Use when writing Dioxus components, signals, effects, resources, server functions, stores, routing, rsx macro code, or fullstack/SSR apps. Also use when the user mentions Dioxus, asks about reactive patterns in Dioxus, or works on any .rs file that imports from dioxus::prelude::*. Even if the question seems simple, consult this skill — Dioxus APIs change frequently between versions and training data is often wrong.
- icu4x: ICU4X 2.x internationalization crate family reference (covers icu_calendar, icu_datetime, icu_plurals, icu_decimal, icu_collator, icu_segmenter, icu_locale, icu_provider, icu_experimental, fixed_decimal). Use when writing or reviewing any Rust code that uses ICU4X crates for i18n, l10n, date/time formatting, number formatting, plural rules, collation, text segmentation, or locale handling. Also use when the user mentions ICU4X, works on any .rs file that imports from icu::* or icu_*::*, or reviews spec files referencing ICU4X APIs. Even if the question seems simple, consult this skill — ICU4X had a major breaking redesign from 1.x to 2.x and training data is almost certainly wrong.
- leptos: Leptos 0.8 reactive web framework reference (covers leptos, leptos_router, reactive_stores, server_fn, leptos_meta). Use when writing Leptos components, signals, effects, resources, actions, server functions, stores, routing, view macro code, or SSR/hydration. Also use when the user mentions Leptos, asks about reactive patterns in Leptos, or works on any .rs file that imports from leptos::*. Even if the question seems simple, consult this skill — Leptos APIs change frequently and training data is often wrong.
- playwright-cli: Automates browser interactions for web testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction. Use when the user needs to navigate websites, interact with web pages, fill forms, take screenshots, test web applications, or extract information from web pages.
- post-implementation-audit: MANDATORY after every implementation task — runs three sequential audits on the new code (spec/impl drift, iterative "anything missing?", test coverage) and lands every finding in the same PR before the user-review step. Invoke as soon as an implementation task's named tests pass, OR when any of these phrases appears: "ready to present", "implementation is complete", "named tests pass", "before opening the PR", "ready for review". Skipping this audit leaves spec drift, untested defensive code, missing conventions, and silent contract violations in the merged PR.
- waiting-for-codex-review: Use immediately after pushing a PR to GitHub when Codex is configured to review the branch. Codex reviews asynchronously — it leaves a 👀 reaction on the PR while reviewing, drops the 👀 when the run finishes (either replacing it with 👍 if satisfied, or removing it and posting review threads if changes are needed). This skill defines the polling loop and the response workflow: wait for Codex's verdict, address any review threads it posts with the same rigor as `post-implementation-audit` (TDD + no coverage regression), commit, push, reply inline, resolve the thread, then keep waiting until 👍. Triggers on phrases like "wait for codex review", "what does codex say", "is codex done", "address the codex feedback", "check the PR reviews", or any post-push handoff where the user expects an automated reviewer's verdict. Invoke this skill even when the user does not explicitly name Codex — any GitHub PR where a 👀/👍 reaction signals an automated reviewer follows the same pattern.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.

- rust-skills:rust-trait-explorer: Explore Rust trait implementations using LSP. Triggers on: /trait-impl, find implementations, who implements, trait 实现, 谁实现了, 实现了哪些trait

Triggers on: unsafe, raw pointer, FFI, extern, transmute, *mut, *const, union, #[repr(C)], libc, std::ffi, MaybeUninit, NonNull, SAFETY comment, soundness, undefined behavior, UB, safe wrapper, memory layout, bindgen, cbindgen, CString, CStr, 安全抽象, 裸指针, 外部函数接口, 内存布局, 不安全代码, FFI 绑定, 未定义行为
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

=== RUST SKILLS DISPLAY FORMAT ===
When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text
2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

content.content:
# Spec Directory Guide

## File Conventions

- `_category.md` files contain category overviews and shared conventions
- Component files use kebab-case matching the component name
- Shared types in `shared/` are referenced by component frontmatter
- Headings are numbered (`## 1. State Machine`, `### 1.1 States`)

## Spec Changes During Implementation

The repo is in active implementation. Spec edits inside `spec/` should usually be tied to a concrete GitHub implementation task or PR, not made as detached cleanup.

- Read the implementation issue before changing spec text.
- Keep cited spec sections synchronized with the implementation task that discovered the mismatch.
- If an adapter-specific implementation exposes a missing shared abstraction, promote that abstraction into the appropriate foundation/shared spec instead of documenting it only in framework code.
- Do not leave placeholder notes for later reconciliation; resolve the spec/code mismatch in the same task whenever possible.

## Adding a New Component

1. Create `components/{category}/{component-name}.md` with YAML frontmatter
2. Follow the section structure defined in `foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` for the appropriate tier
3. Add an entry to `manifest.toml` with path, category, deps, and related
4. Update `foundation/02-component-catalog.md` with the new component
5. Run `cargo run -p spec-tool -- validate` to verify consistency

## Component File Format

```markdown
---
component: ComponentName
category: { category }
tier: stateless | stateful | complex
foundation_deps: [architecture, accessibility, ...]
shared_deps: [date-time-types, ...]
related: [sibling-component, ...]
---

# ComponentName
```

See `foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` for the canonical section structure, ordering rules, conformance checklist, and skeleton examples for each tier.

## Summary Report: ars-components Specialized Module Structure

### 1. Specialized Module Organization

The `specialized` module is located at `/Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-294/crates/ars-components/src/specialized/` and uses a **directory-backed mod.rs pattern** where each component lives in its own subdirectory.

**Existing Specialized Components (13 total):**
- `angle_slider` - circular angle-input machine
- `clipboard` - stateful clipboard copy operation machine
- `color_area` - 2D color-picker machine
- `color_field` - color-value text input machine
- `color_picker` - complex orchestrator machine
- `color_slider` - 1D single-channel color slider machine
- `color_swatch` - stateless color-preview connect API
- `color_swatch_picker` - listbox-of-swatches machine
- `color_wheel` - circular hue-picker machine
- `contextual_help` - composition API over popover
- `file_upload` - file upload queue machine
- `qr_code` - stateless QR-matrix rendering connect API

### 2. Representative Stateful Component: Clipboard

The **clipboard** component is the best representative of a stateful specialized component with timer/interval effects. It uses:
- **State enum** with 4 states (Idle, Copying, Copied, Error)
- **Event enum** with Copy, CopySuccess, CopyError, ResetTimeout, SetValue, SetProps
- **Effect enum** with named effects: WriteText, FeedbackTimer, AnnounceCopied, AnnounceError
- **Context struct** storing bindable value, duration, disabled flag, error state, locale, and component IDs
- **Props struct** with builder methods
- **Machine impl** with transition() and on_props_changed()
- **Part enum** with ComponentPart derive
- **Api struct** implementing ConnectApi
- **PendingEffect** instances with named effects and cleanup callbacks

**Key aspects:**
- FeedbackTimer effect dispatches ResetTimeout after feedback_duration_ms
- Effect cancellation: `.cancel_effect(Effect::FeedbackTimer)` when re-triggering
- no_cleanup() function for effects that don't require cleanup

**Complete file:** `/Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-294/crates/ars-components/src/specialized/clipboard/mod.rs` (1310 lines, shown above in full)

### 3. Component Registration & Re-exports

**File: `/Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-294/crates/ars-components/src/specialized/mod.rs`**

```rust
//! Specialized component machines.

pub mod angle_slider;
pub mod clipboard;
pub mod color_area;
// ... etc
pub mod qr_code;
```

Each component is exported as a public module. The pattern is simple: one line per component.

**File: `/Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-294/crates/ars-components/src/lib.rs`**

```rust
pub mod specialized;
```

The specialized module is re-exported at the crate root.

### 4. Directory Structure for New Timer Component

Following the established pattern, for a new `timer` component you would create:

**Directory structure:**
```
/Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-294/crates/ars-components/src/specialized/timer/
├── mod.rs                    (main component implementation, ~1000-1500 lines)
└── snapshots/               (auto-generated by insta for snapshot tests)
```

**Files to create:**
1. `/Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-294/crates/ars-components/src/specialized/timer/mod.rs` - Main component file
2. (Optional) `/Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-294/crates/ars-components/src/specialized/timer/tests.rs` - If test code gets large (like file_upload does)

**Module registration changes:**
1. Add to `/Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-294/crates/ars-components/src/specialized/mod.rs`:
   ```rust
   /// `Timer` countdown/interval machine and connect API.
   ```

content.text: This is the canonical pattern. Toast's `DurationTimer` marker effect is owned by the adapter, emitted via `initial_effects` on mount and `with_effect` on resume, cancelled via `cancel_effect`. Timer should follow this exactly. Let me check how the spec-conformance and proptest test suites register component modules so I wire Timer in correctly.

content.text:
I now have complete clarity. The implementation follows the well-established Clipboard/Toast marker-effect pattern. There's one notable spec/code mismatch to resolve (the spec §1.5 machine impl uses string effect names and nonexistent `set_interval`/`cancel_interval`/inline `use_platform_effects().announce()`, none of which match the current `Effect`-enum + marker-effect convention). CLAUDE.md mandates resolving that in the same PR.

Let me write the plan.

input.content:
# Task #294 — Implement Timer agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #294 (Epic #227, Agnostic specialized components) asks for the framework-agnostic
core of a **Timer** component: a stateful machine with `Idle / Running / Paused / Completed`
states, countdown + stopwatch modes, a configurable tick interval, auto-start, and a `ConnectApi`
that emits `role="timer"` with an `aria-label`, a `progressbar`, and segment/formatted-time output.
Adapters (Leptos/Dioxus) are explicitly **out of scope**. Test tier: Unit. 3 points.

The spec lives at `spec/components/specialized/timer.md` (tier: stateful, deps:
architecture/accessibility/interactions). The component does not exist in code yet.

### Spec drift that MUST be resolved in this PR

The spec's §1.5 machine implementation predates the workspace's current effect conventions and
will not compile as written. CLAUDE.md requires resolving spec/code mismatches in the same task,
so this PR will both implement the component to current conventions **and** rewrite the drifted
spec sections to match. Three concrete drifts:

1. **String effect names** (`with_named_effect("timer-interval", …)`, `"announce"`). Current
   `ars_core::Machine` requires `type Effect: EffectName` — a typed enum. (`EffectName` is a
   blanket impl over `Copy + Debug + Eq + Hash + Send + Sync + 'static`, satisfied by a plain
   `#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]` enum, exactly like `clipboard::Effect` and
   `toast::single::Effect`.)
2. **`set_interval` / `cancel_interval` do not exist** anywhere, and `PlatformEffects` exposes only
   `set_timeout` (no recurring interval). The recurring tick must be a **marker effect** owned by
   the adapter — the precedent is `toast::single::Effect::DurationTimer` and
   `clipboard::Effect::FeedbackTimer`, where the agnostic core emits a named intent and the adapter
   wires the real `setInterval`, dispatching `Event::Tick` each interval.
3. **Inline `use_platform_effects().announce(...)`** inside an effect closure — `use_platform_effects`
   is adapter-only. Completion announcement becomes a marker effect (precedent:
   `clipboard::Effect::AnnounceCopied`, `toast::Effect::AnnouncePolite`).

## Files to create / modify

### New: `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/timer/mod.rs`
Directory-backed module (`timer/mod.rs` + `timer/snapshots/`), mirroring
`crates/ars-components/src/specialized/clipboard/mod.rs` structure. Contents:

- `pub enum Mode { Countdown (default), Stopwatch }` — `#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]`.
- `pub enum State { Idle, Running, Paused, Completed }` — per spec §1.1.
- `pub enum Event { Start, Pause, Resume, Reset, Restart, Tick, SetTime(u64) }` — per spec §1.2.
- `pub struct Context { current_ms, target_ms, interval_ms, mode, auto_start, locale, messages, ids }` — spec §1.3.
- `pub struct Props { id, target_ms, interval_ms, mode, auto_start }` + `Default` (target 60_000,
  interval 1000, Countdown, no auto-start) + builder methods (`new`, `id`, `target_ms`,
  `interval_ms`, `mode`, `auto_start`) following clipboard's `#[must_use]` builder style. `#[derive(… HasId)]`.
- `pub struct Messages { start_label, pause_label, reset_label, resume_label, progress_label,
  completed_announcement }` with `MessageFn::static_str` defaults (spec §4.1) + `impl ComponentMessages`.
- **`pub enum Effect { TimerInterval, AnnounceCompleted }`** (replaces spec's string names), each
  documented with its adapter contract:
  - `TimerInterval` — adapter starts a recurring interval of `ctx.interval_ms` ms dispatching
    `Event::Tick`; cancelled whenever the timer leaves `Running` (adapter no-ops if none active).
  - `AnnounceCompleted` — adapter announces `messages.completed_announcement` into a polite live region.
- `pub struct Machine; impl ars_core::Machine for Machine` with:
  - `init` — current_ms = target (countdown) / 0 (stopwatch); initial state Running iff `auto_start`
    else Idle (spec §1.5).
  - `initial_effects` — when booted into `Running` (auto-start), emit `PendingEffect::named(Effect::TimerInterval)`
    (precedent: `toast::single::initial_effects`). This is how auto-start starts ticking without a transition.
  - `transition` — `(Idle, Start)` → Running + `with_effect(named TimerInterval)`;
    `(Running, Tick)` countdown: `saturating_sub(interval)`; if 0 → Completed (`apply` current_ms=0)
    `.cancel_effect(TimerInterval).with_effect(named AnnounceCompleted)`, else `context_only` decrement;
    `(Running, Tick)` stopwatch: `context_only` `saturating_add(interval)` (defensive vs spec's `+=`);
    `(Running, Pause)` → Paused `.cancel_effect(TimerInterval)`;
    `(Paused, Resume | Start)` → Running `.with_effect(named TimerInterval)`;
    `(_, Reset)` → Idle, reset current_ms `.cancel_effect(TimerInterval)`;
    `(_, Restart)` → Running, reset current_ms `.cancel_effect(TimerInterval).with_effect(named TimerInterval)`;
    `(_, SetTime(ms))` → `context_only` set current_ms; else `None`.
  - `connect` — build `Api`.
- `#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "timer"] pub enum Part { Root, Label, Display, Progress,
  StartTrigger, PauseTrigger, ResetTrigger, Separator }` (spec §1.6 / §2; 8 parts).
- `pub struct Api<'a>` + manual `Debug` (like clipboard) with: query accessors (`is_running`,
  `is_paused`, `is_completed`, `is_idle`, `current_ms`), `display_time`, `progress`,
  `formatted_time`, private `state_str`, all `*_attrs()` methods (spec §1.6 verbatim semantics:
  Root `role="timer"` + `aria-live=polite` + `aria-atomic` + `aria-label`=formatted_time +
  `data-ars-state`; Progress `role="progressbar"` + valuenow/min/max + label + custom style;
  triggers with state-dependent `aria-label` + `disabled`; Display/Separator `aria-hidden`),
  click handlers (`on_start_trigger_click`, `on_pause_trigger_click`, `on_reset_trigger_click`,
  `on_restart`), and `impl ConnectApi` dispatching `part_attrs`.

### Modify: `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/mod.rs`
Add `pub mod timer;` (doc-commented one-liner, alphabetical-ish with siblings).

### Modify: `spec/components/specialized/timer.md` (spec sync)
Rewrite §1.2-ish effect references and §1.5 machine impl to the `Effect`-enum + marker-effect model;
add an `Effect` enum block + adapter-contract docs; remove `set_interval`/`cancel_interval` and the
inline `use_platform_effects().announce()`; note auto-start emits `TimerInterval` via `initial_effects`.
Keep §1.1/1.3/1.4/1.6 API surface, §2 anatomy, §3 a11y, §4 i18n, §5 parity unchanged in contract.

### Tests (TDD — write first)

- **Unit + snapshot** (inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in `timer/mod.rs`): mirror clipboard. Cover
  every named test from the issue — full transition path `Idle→Running→Paused→Running→Completed`;
  countdown decrements + reaches Completed with `AnnounceCompleted`; stopwatch increments from 0;
  configurable `interval_ms`; auto-start (boots Running + `take_initial_effects` yields
  `TimerInterval`); `SetTime`; `Reset`/`Restart` cancel/restart the interval; props defaults/builder;
  Api accessors + `display_time`/`progress`/`formatted_time`; effect emission/cancellation via
  `SendResult.pending_effects` / `cancel_effects` (clipboard precedent). Snapshot `*_attrs()` for
  every part across output-affecting states (idle/running/paused/completed), committed `.snap`
  fixtures (`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`). Use `Service::<Machine>::new(props,
  &Env::default(), &Messages::default())` and the `Box::leak` `api_for_state` helper from clipboard.
- **Spec-conformance**: add `timer_anatomy_matches_spec` to
  `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/specialized.rs` via `assert_anatomy("timer", &[…8 parts…])`.
- **Proptest**: add a `mod timer_proptests` to
  `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/specialized.rs` (mirror `color_area_proptests`):
  arbitrary event sequences, asserting invariants — `current_ms` never exceeds `target_ms` in
  countdown / never goes negative; state⇔effect consistency (no `TimerInterval` active after leaving
  Running); `#![proptest_config(crate::common::proptest_config())]`, `#[ignore = "proptest — nightly
  extended-proptest job"]`.

## Reused existing APIs (verified)
- `ars_core::{Machine, TransitionPlan, PendingEffect, no_cleanup, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages,
  ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env, HasId, HtmlAttr, AriaAttr, AttrMap, CssProperty, MessageFn, Locale}`.
- `TransitionPlan::{to, context_only, apply, with_effect, cancel_effect}`,
  `PendingEffect::named`, `Service::{new, send, set_props, connect, take_initial_effects,
  state, context, props}`, `SendResult.{state_changed, context_changed, pending_effects, cancel_effects}`.
- Pattern precedents: `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/clipboard/mod.rs` (structure, tests,
  marker `FeedbackTimer`/`AnnounceCopied`), `crates/ars-components/src/overlay/toast/single.rs`
  (`DurationTimer` marker timer + `initial_effects` for auto-started timer).

## Post-implementation gate (mandatory, per CLAUDE.md step 7)
After named tests pass, run the `post-implementation-audit` skill (spec-drift, "anything missing"
×2, test-coverage) and land every finding in this PR.

## Verification
1. `cargo test -p ars-components timer` (unit + snapshot) — all green, zero warnings.
2. `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` — snapshots committed/clean.
3. `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance specialized::timer` — anatomy matches.
4. `PROPTEST_CASES=1000 cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines -- --ignored timer` — invariants hold.
5. `cargo xtask spec validate` — spec frontmatter still consistent after spec edits.
6. After user approval: `cargo xci-fast`, then commit + PR to `main` with `Closes #294`,
   `snapshot-reviewed` label (new `.snap` files), then `waiting-for-codex-review` loop.

lastPrompt: Implement task #294

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/ericson/.claude/plans/implement-task-294-harmonic-stallman.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.plan:
# Task #294 — Implement Timer agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #294 (Epic #227, Agnostic specialized components) asks for the framework-agnostic
core of a **Timer** component: a stateful machine with `Idle / Running / Paused / Completed`
states, countdown + stopwatch modes, a configurable tick interval, auto-start, and a `ConnectApi`
that emits `role="timer"` with an `aria-label`, a `progressbar`, and segment/formatted-time output.
Adapters (Leptos/Dioxus) are explicitly **out of scope**. Test tier: Unit. 3 points.

The spec lives at `spec/components/specialized/timer.md` (tier: stateful, deps:
architecture/accessibility/interactions). The component does not exist in code yet.

### Spec drift that MUST be resolved in this PR

The spec's §1.5 machine implementation predates the workspace's current effect conventions and
will not compile as written. CLAUDE.md requires resolving spec/code mismatches in the same task,
so this PR will both implement the component to current conventions **and** rewrite the drifted
spec sections to match. Three concrete drifts:

1. **String effect names** (`with_named_effect("timer-interval", …)`, `"announce"`). Current
   `ars_core::Machine` requires `type Effect: EffectName` — a typed enum. (`EffectName` is a
   blanket impl over `Copy + Debug + Eq + Hash + Send + Sync + 'static`, satisfied by a plain
   `#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]` enum, exactly like `clipboard::Effect` and
   `toast::single::Effect`.)
2. **`set_interval` / `cancel_interval` do not exist** anywhere, and `PlatformEffects` exposes only
   `set_timeout` (no recurring interval). The recurring tick must be a **marker effect** owned by
   the adapter — the precedent is `toast::single::Effect::DurationTimer` and
   `clipboard::Effect::FeedbackTimer`, where the agnostic core emits a named intent and the adapter
   wires the real `setInterval`, dispatching `Event::Tick` each interval.
3. **Inline `use_platform_effects().announce(...)`** inside an effect closure — `use_platform_effects`
   is adapter-only. Completion announcement becomes a marker effect (precedent:
   `clipboard::Effect::AnnounceCopied`, `toast::Effect::AnnouncePolite`).

## Files to create / modify

### New: `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/timer/mod.rs`
Directory-backed module (`timer/mod.rs` + `timer/snapshots/`), mirroring
`crates/ars-components/src/specialized/clipboard/mod.rs` structure. Contents:

- `pub enum Mode { Countdown (default), Stopwatch }` — `#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]`.
- `pub enum State { Idle, Running, Paused, Completed }` — per spec §1.1.
- `pub enum Event { Start, Pause, Resume, Reset, Restart, Tick, SetTime(u64) }` — per spec §1.2.
- `pub struct Context { current_ms, target_ms, interval_ms, mode, auto_start, locale, messages, ids }` — spec §1.3.
- `pub struct Props { id, target_ms, interval_ms, mode, auto_start }` + `Default` (target 60_000,
  interval 1000, Countdown, no auto-start) + builder methods (`new`, `id`, `target_ms`,
  `interval_ms`, `mode`, `auto_start`) following clipboard's `#[must_use]` builder style. `#[derive(… HasId)]`.
- `pub struct Messages { start_label, pause_label, reset_label, resume_label, progress_label,
  completed_announcement }` with `MessageFn::static_str` defaults (spec §4.1) + `impl ComponentMessages`.
- **`pub enum Effect { TimerInterval, AnnounceCompleted }`** (replaces spec's string names), each
  documented with its adapter contract:
  - `TimerInterval` — adapter starts a recurring interval of `ctx.interval_ms` ms dispatching
    `Event::Tick`; cancelled whenever the timer leaves `Running` (adapter no-ops if none active).
  - `AnnounceCompleted` — adapter announces `messages.completed_announcement` into a polite live region.
- `pub struct Machine; impl ars_core::Machine for Machine` with:
  - `init` — current_ms = target (countdown) / 0 (stopwatch); initial state Running iff `auto_start`
    else Idle (spec §1.5).
  - `initial_effects` — when booted into `Running` (auto-start), emit `PendingEffect::named(Effect::TimerInterval)`
    (precedent: `toast::single::initial_effects`). This is how auto-start starts ticking without a transition.
  - `transition` — `(Idle, Start)` → Running + `with_effect(named TimerInterval)`;
    `(Running, Tick)` countdown: `saturating_sub(interval)`; if 0 → Completed (`apply` current_ms=0)
    `.cancel_effect(TimerInterval).with_effect(named AnnounceCompleted)`, else `context_only` decrement;
    `(Running, Tick)` stopwatch: `context_only` `saturating_add(interval)` (defensive vs spec's `+=`);
    `(Running, Pause)` → Paused `.cancel_effect(TimerInterval)`;
    `(Paused, Resume | Start)` → Running `.with_effect(named TimerInterval)`;
    `(_, Reset)` → Idle, reset current_ms `.cancel_effect(TimerInterval)`;
    `(_, Restart)` → Running, reset current_ms `.cancel_effect(TimerInterval).with_effect(named TimerInterval)`;
    `(_, SetTime(ms))` → `context_only` set current_ms; else `None`.
  - `connect` — build `Api`.
- `#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "timer"] pub enum Part { Root, Label, Display, Progress,
  StartTrigger, PauseTrigger, ResetTrigger, Separator }` (spec §1.6 / §2; 8 parts).
- `pub struct Api<'a>` + manual `Debug` (like clipboard) with: query accessors (`is_running`,
  `is_paused`, `is_completed`, `is_idle`, `current_ms`), `display_time`, `progress`,
  `formatted_time`, private `state_str`, all `*_attrs()` methods (spec §1.6 verbatim semantics:
  Root `role="timer"` + `aria-live=polite` + `aria-atomic` + `aria-label`=formatted_time +
  `data-ars-state`; Progress `role="progressbar"` + valuenow/min/max + label + custom style;
  triggers with state-dependent `aria-label` + `disabled`; Display/Separator `aria-hidden`),
  click handlers (`on_start_trigger_click`, `on_pause_trigger_click`, `on_reset_trigger_click`,
  `on_restart`), and `impl ConnectApi` dispatching `part_attrs`.

### Modify: `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/mod.rs`
Add `pub mod timer;` (doc-commented one-liner, alphabetical-ish with siblings).

### Modify: `spec/components/specialized/timer.md` (spec sync)
Rewrite §1.2-ish effect references and §1.5 machine impl to the `Effect`-enum + marker-effect model;
add an `Effect` enum block + adapter-contract docs; remove `set_interval`/`cancel_interval` and the
inline `use_platform_effects().announce()`; note auto-start emits `TimerInterval` via `initial_effects`.
Keep §1.1/1.3/1.4/1.6 API surface, §2 anatomy, §3 a11y, §4 i18n, §5 parity unchanged in contract.

### Tests (TDD — write first)

- **Unit + snapshot** (inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in `timer/mod.rs`): mirror clipboard. Cover
  every named test from the issue — full transition path `Idle→Running→Paused→Running→Completed`;
  countdown decrements + reaches Completed with `AnnounceCompleted`; stopwatch increments from 0;
  configurable `interval_ms`; auto-start (boots Running + `take_initial_effects` yields
  `TimerInterval`); `SetTime`; `Reset`/`Restart` cancel/restart the interval; props defaults/builder;
  Api accessors + `display_time`/`progress`/`formatted_time`; effect emission/cancellation via
  `SendResult.pending_effects` / `cancel_effects` (clipboard precedent). Snapshot `*_attrs()` for
  every part across output-affecting states (idle/running/paused/completed), committed `.snap`
  fixtures (`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`). Use `Service::<Machine>::new(props,
  &Env::default(), &Messages::default())` and the `Box::leak` `api_for_state` helper from clipboard.
- **Spec-conformance**: add `timer_anatomy_matches_spec` to
  `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/specialized.rs` via `assert_anatomy("timer", &[…8 parts…])`.
- **Proptest**: add a `mod timer_proptests` to
  `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/specialized.rs` (mirror `color_area_proptests`):
  arbitrary event sequences, asserting invariants — `current_ms` never exceeds `target_ms` in
  countdown / never goes negative; state⇔effect consistency (no `TimerInterval` active after leaving
  Running); `#![proptest_config(crate::common::proptest_config())]`, `#[ignore = "proptest — nightly
  extended-proptest job"]`.

## Reused existing APIs (verified)
- `ars_core::{Machine, TransitionPlan, PendingEffect, no_cleanup, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages,
  ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env, HasId, HtmlAttr, AriaAttr, AttrMap, CssProperty, MessageFn, Locale}`.
- `TransitionPlan::{to, context_only, apply, with_effect, cancel_effect}`,
  `PendingEffect::named`, `Service::{new, send, set_props, connect, take_initial_effects,
  state, context, props}`, `SendResult.{state_changed, context_changed, pending_effects, cancel_effects}`.
- Pattern precedents: `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/clipboard/mod.rs` (structure, tests,
  marker `FeedbackTimer`/`AnnounceCopied`), `crates/ars-components/src/overlay/toast/single.rs`
  (`DurationTimer` marker timer + `initial_effects` for auto-started timer).

## Post-implementation gate (mandatory, per CLAUDE.md step 7)
After named tests pass, run the `post-implementation-audit` skill (spec-drift, "anything missing"
×2, test-coverage) and land every finding in this PR.

## Verification
1. `cargo test -p ars-components timer` (unit + snapshot) — all green, zero warnings.
2. `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` — snapshots committed/clean.
3. `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance specialized::timer` — anatomy matches.
4. `PROPTEST_CASES=1000 cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines -- --ignored timer` — invariants hold.
5. `cargo xtask spec validate` — spec frontmatter still consistent after spec edits.
6. After user approval: `cargo xci-fast`, then commit + PR to `main` with `Closes #294`,
   `snapshot-reviewed` label (new `.snap` files), then `waiting-for-codex-review` loop.

- `pub struct Api<'a>` + manual `Debug` (like clipboard) with: query accessors (`is_running`,
  `is_paused`, `is_completed`, `is_idle`, `current_ms`), `display_time`, `progress`,
  `formatted_time`, private `state_str`, all `*_attrs()` methods (spec §1.6 verbatim semantics:
  Root `role="timer"` + `aria-live=polite` + `aria-atomic` + `aria-label`=formatted_time +
  `data-ars-state`; Progress `role="progressbar"` + valuenow/min/max + label + custom style;
  triggers with state-dependent `aria-label` + `disabled`; Display/Separator `aria-hidden`),
  click handlers (`on_start_trigger_click`, `on_pause_trigger_click`, `on_reset_trigger_click`,
  `on_restart`), and `impl ConnectApi` dispatching `part_attrs`.

### Modify: `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/mod.rs`
Add `pub mod timer;` (doc-commented one-liner, alphabetical-ish with siblings).

### Modify: `spec/components/specialized/timer.md` (spec sync)
Rewrite §1.2-ish effect references and §1.5 machine impl to the `Effect`-enum + marker-effect model;
add an `Effect` enum block + adapter-contract docs; remove `set_interval`/`cancel_interval` and the
inline `use_platform_effects().announce()`; note auto-start emits `TimerInterval` via `initial_effects`.
Keep §1.1/1.3/1.4/1.6 API surface, §2 anatomy, §3 a11y, §4 i18n, §5 parity unchanged in contract.

### Tests (TDD — write first)

- **Unit + snapshot** (inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in `timer/mod.rs`): mirror clipboard. Cover
  every named test from the issue — full transition path `Idle→Running→Paused→Running→Completed`;
  countdown decrements + reaches Completed with `AnnounceCompleted`; stopwatch increments from 0;
  configurable `interval_ms`; auto-start (boots Running + `take_initial_effects` yields
  `TimerInterval`); `SetTime`; `Reset`/`Restart` cancel/restart the interval; props defaults/builder;
  Api accessors + `display_time`/`progress`/`formatted_time`; effect emission/cancellation via
  `SendResult.pending_effects` / `cancel_effects` (clipboard precedent). Snapshot `*_attrs()` for
  every part across output-affecting states (idle/running/paused/completed), committed `.snap`
  fixtures (`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`). Use `Service::<Machine>::new(props,
  &Env::default(), &Messages::default())` and the `Box::leak` `api_for_state` helper from clipboard.
- **Spec-conformance**: add `timer_anatomy_matches_spec` to
  `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/specialized.rs` via `assert_anatomy("timer", &[…8 parts…])`.
- **Proptest**: add a `mod timer_proptests` to
  `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/specialized.rs` (mirror `color_area_proptests`):
  arbitrary event sequences, asserting invariants — `current_ms` never exceeds `target_ms` in
  countdown / never goes negative; state⇔effect consistency (no `TimerInterval` active after leaving
  Running); `#![proptest_config(crate::common::proptest_config())]`, `#[ignore = "proptest — nightly
  extended-proptest job"]`.

## Reused existing APIs (verified)
- `ars_core::{Machine, TransitionPlan, PendingEffect, no_cleanup, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages,
  ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env, HasId, HtmlAttr, AriaAttr, AttrMap, CssProperty, MessageFn, Locale}`.
- `TransitionPlan::{to, context_only, apply, with_effect, cancel_effect}`,
  `PendingEffect::named`, `Service::{new, send, set_props, connect, take_initial_effects,
  state, context, props}`, `SendResult.{state_changed, context_changed, pending_effects, cancel_effects}`.
- Pattern precedents: `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/clipboard/mod.rs` (structure, tests,
  marker `FeedbackTimer`/`AnnounceCopied`), `crates/ars-components/src/overlay/toast/single.rs`
  (`DurationTimer` marker timer + `initial_effects` for auto-started timer).

## Post-implementation gate (mandatory, per CLAUDE.md step 7)
After named tests pass, run the `post-implementation-audit` skill (spec-drift, "anything missing"
×2, test-coverage) and land every finding in this PR.

## Verification
1. `cargo test -p ars-components timer` (unit + snapshot) — all green, zero warnings.
2. `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` — snapshots committed/clean.
3. `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance specialized::timer` — anatomy matches.
4. `PROPTEST_CASES=1000 cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines -- --ignored timer` — invariants hold.
5. `cargo xtask spec validate` — spec frontmatter still consistent after spec edits.
6. After user approval: `cargo xci-fast`, then commit + PR to `main` with `Closes #294`,
   `snapshot-reviewed` label (new `.snap` files), then `waiting-for-codex-review` loop.

content.text: The proptest found a real semantic detail: `SetTime` is unconditional (`(_, SetTime)`), so it can set a nonzero `current_ms` while the machine stays in `Completed`. That's correct per-spec behavior (matches the existing `clipboard`/`toast` unconditional context-sync pattern), so my per-step invariant was too strong. The completion-at-zero guarantee is already covered by the unit test (`timer_full_lifecycle...`). Let me relax that proptest invariant.

content.content:
1	//! `spec compile-snippets` — parse Rust code blocks in spec markdown
2	//! through `syn::parse_file` and surface syntax errors.
3	//!
4	//! ## What this catches
5	//!
6	//! - Top-level Rust syntax bugs in spec snippets: missing braces,
7	//!   mistyped `pub fn`, unterminated string literals, unbalanced
8	//!   generics outside macro bodies, etc.
9	//! - Coverage across the full spec corpus —
10	//!   `spec/{components,foundation,leptos-components,dioxus-components,shared,testing}/`.
11	//!   The pre-existing `lint-code` linter only walked
12	//!   `spec/components/` and never invoked a Rust parser, so foundation
13	//!   and per-adapter specs had zero parse coverage.
14	//!
15	//! ## What this **does not** catch (important — read this)
16	//!
17	//! - **Macro-internal syntax.** `view! { … }` and `rsx! { … }` are
18	//!   opaque token trees to both `syn` and `rustc`; everything inside
19	//!   the outer braces is unchecked. The original Leptos `view!`
20	//!   regression (an inline `|errors: ArcRwSignal<Errors>|` closure
21	//!   where the macro parser misread `<Errors>` as an HTML tag) sits
22	//!   *inside* the macro and would not be caught here. The unit test
23	//!   suites at `crates/ars-{leptos,dioxus}/tests/` are the source of
24	//!   truth for macro-internal correctness.
25	//! - **Type / path resolution.** A snippet that names a type defined
26	//!   nowhere in the workspace still parses syntactically. Use
27	//!   `cargo check` against a fixture crate for that.
28	//! - **Borrow-checker / ownership errors.** Same as above —
29	//!   `syn::parse_file` is purely a parser.
30	//!
31	//! ## Opting out
32	//!
33	//! Code blocks that intentionally contain partial or pseudo-Rust
34	//! (signatures shown for documentation purposes, top-level expressions
35	//! meant to live inside an outer fn body, etc.) opt out by tagging the
36	//! fence info-string with `rust,no_check` (also recognised:
37	//! `rust,ignore`, `rust,no_run`, mirroring rustdoc). The extractor
38	//! honors those markers and skips the block.
39	//!
40	//! ## Performance
41	//!
42	//! Fast: no compiler invocation. Runs in milliseconds across all 2000+
43	//! Rust blocks in the spec, so it is suitable for inclusion in the
44	//! `cargo xci` pipeline (see `xtask::ci::Step::SpecCompileSnippets`).
45	
46	use std::{fs, path::Path};
47	
48	use crate::manifest::{Error, SpecRoot};
49	
50	/// CLI entry point.
51	///
52	/// Always returns `Ok(String)`. The text either begins with `ok:` (no
53	/// findings) or with `N finding(s):` followed by per-finding lines.
54	///
55	/// When `fix` is `true`, each failing block has its opening fence rewritten
56	/// from `` ```rust `` (or `` ```rs ``) to `` ```rust,no_check `` (or
57	/// `` ```rs,no_check ``) in place; the file is rewritten on disk and the
58	/// returned text reports how many fences were patched.
59	///
60	/// # Errors
61	///
62	/// Returns [`Error::Io`] when a target file cannot be read or written.
63	pub fn execute(root: &SpecRoot, fix: bool) -> Result<String, Error> {
64	    let mut files = Vec::new();
65	
66	    for sub in [
67	        "components",
68	        "foundation",
69	        "leptos-components",
70	        "dioxus-components",
71	        "shared",
72	        "testing",
73	    ] {
74	        let dir = root.path.join(sub);
75	
76	        collect_md_files(&dir, &mut files).map_err(Error::Io)?;
77	    }
78	
79	    files.sort();
80	
81	    let display_root = fs::canonicalize(&root.path).unwrap_or_else(|_| root.path.clone());
82	
83	    let mut findings = Vec::new();
84	    let mut blocks_checked = 0usize;
85	    let mut fences_patched = 0usize;
86	
87	    for path in &files {
88	        let source = fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(Error::Io)?;
89	
90	        let display = path
91	            .strip_prefix(&display_root)
92	            .unwrap_or(path)
93	            .display()
94	            .to_string();
95	
96	        let blocks = extract_rust_blocks(&source);
97	
98	        let mut failing_fence_lines = Vec::new();
99	
100	        for block in &blocks {
101	            blocks_checked += 1;
102	
103	            if let Some(message) = check_block(block) {
104	                findings.push(Finding {
105	                    file: display.clone(),
106	                    line: block.start_line,
107	                    message,
108	                });
109	
110	                if fix {
111	                    // The fence line is one above the block's first content line.
112	                    failing_fence_lines.push(block.start_line.saturating_sub(1));
113	                }
114	            }
115	        }
116	
117	        if fix && !failing_fence_lines.is_empty() {
118	            let patched = rewrite_fences_to_no_check(&source, &failing_fence_lines);
119	
120	            if patched != source {
121	                let count = patched_fence_count(&source, &patched);
122	
123	                fences_patched += count;
124	
125	                fs::write(path, patched).map_err(Error::Io)?;
126	            }
127	        }
128	    }
129	
130	    let mut out = String::new();
131	
132	    if findings.is_empty() {
133	        out.push_str(&format!(
134	            "ok: {} block(s) parsed across {} spec file(s), no syntax findings\n",
135	            blocks_checked,
136	            files.len()
137	        ));
138	
139	        return Ok(out);
140	    }
141	
142	    if fix {
143	        // Re-run after patching so the report reflects the fixed state and
144	        // exit-code pipelines see a clean run on the next invocation.
145	        out.push_str(&format!(
146	            "fixed: patched {fences_patched} fence(s) across {} spec file(s) ({blocks_checked} \
147	             block(s) parsed). Re-run `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets` to confirm a clean \
148	             pass.\n",
149	            files.len()
150	        ));
151	
152	        return Ok(out);
153	    }
154	
155	    out.push_str(&format!(
156	        "{} finding(s) across {} spec file(s) ({} block(s) parsed):\n\n",
157	        findings.len(),
158	        files.len(),
159	        blocks_checked,
160	    ));
